codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `93b4f4b3`. The rule is: split the input into equal left/right halves, match each colored right-side component to the left-side zero hole with the same geometry, fill that hole with the component’s color, and return only the completed left half.

The `opus` summary hint was used as a starting hypothesis and it matched the official examples; the `sonnet` file had no entry for this task. The generator stays on-family by building the same stacked-strip layout seen in the puzzle: a framed left panel with zero holes, a black right panel with connected motifs in the same strip geometry, and a non-identity vertical permutation between sides. No new primitive was needed.

Verification passed end to end: official examples `3/3`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I visually checked a generated preview sheet.
